terms.definition: Without action, inaction. State of avoiding duty through laziness, fear, or false renunciation; hacker metaphor: A process stopped but still consuming resources.
not disturbed, undisturbed; lit. un-struck, un-violated — in Sāṅkhya terms, the puruṣa (conscious witness) remains unaffected by the perpetual injections of prakṛtic activity
terms.roots: from pāpa (strike) + viddha (struck) with privative a-

terms.definition: Detachment: Operating without the hooks of expectation. Recognition that the actor and the fruits are in different categories, even while the action continues.
terms.roots: from saj (to cling, attach) with privative a-

terms.definition: Indwelling Kernel: The paramātmā embedded in your consciousness stack; the Supreme residing within the heart of every living entity.

Imperishable, inexhaustible. The soul's fundamental quality: it cannot be diminished by birth, confounded by death, or fragmented across time. Like root access to the system — credentials that persist across reboots.
terms.roots: from a (privative prefix) + vya (to decay, perish)

terms.definition: Liberated from bondage; the deletion of the karmic link between action and actor.
O descendant of Bharata — appellation invoking Arjuna's paternal lineage; reminder of the greatness of the Bharata dynasty
Turned to ashes; complete erasure of legacy karmic debt. In Sāṅkhya: the dissolution of the false identification of the soul with the material products of action.
Placing one's actions into the Absolute; transferring ownership of the result to the Supreme.

Birth. In classical Sāṅkhya: not the origin of the soul (which is eternal), but the assumption of a new instrument — the body-mind complex — through which puruṣa falsely identifies as agent and enjoyer. The phala (fruit) of prakṛti's cycle, binding until knowledge dissolves the bond.
terms.roots: from jan (to be born, to arise)

terms.definition: Conquered senses; mastery over the impulses of mind and body.
terms.roots: from jita (conquered) + indriya (senses, organs of perception and action)

terms.definition: The fire of knowledge; the ultimate debugging tool that removes the illusion of separation. A root-level system wipe that clears all corrupted legacy partitions. Not a conceptual understanding, but a transformative realization that consumes the fuel of desire.
Action, work, performance of duty. From root kr (to do); hacker metaphor: The executable—action that produces output in the system.
O son of Kuntī — appellation invoking Arjuna's maternal lineage; reminder of the duty that comes from noble birth
The yoga of action; Patanjali's term (YS II.1) for the path integrating tapas, svādhyāya, and īśvara-praṇidhānana.
Root Portal: The lunar mansion governed by Nirṛti (dissolution). Your BIOS reset point.
Not sticking; the state of the soul that remains untouched by karmic residue, like a lotus leaf in water.
Free from dualities; the state of zero oscillation between attraction and repulsion.
The permanent renouncer; one whose internal state is permanently decoupled from external results.
Prescribed, regulated, bound by duty. Dharma-specific action aligned with one's position; hacker metaphor: The code compiled for your specific architecture.
terms.roots: from ni (down, into) + yam (to restrain, regulate)

terms.definition: The Supreme: For the bhakta, the Personality of Godhead; for the jñānī, liberation. The destination reached when attachment to results is traded for connection to source.

Subduer of the enemy. Arjuna's epithet carries weight here. The enemy to be subdued is not the Kurus on the battlefield — it is the ignorance that makes him forget his nature, his past, his purpose. The warrior who can defeat external armies cannot defeat internal darkness without divine knowledge.
terms.roots: from param (greatest, beyond) + tapa (to heat, to burn)

terms.definition: Pure, sublime, sanctified. The state of being free from the residues of material desire; hacker metaphor: A clean-room environment, free from any external noise or interference.
Sense Withdrawal: The kill -9 command for sensory interrupts. The process of pulling the awareness away from the sense objects and centering it in the mind.
The "Early Rain" nakṣatra; symbolizes the flow of will being redirected from the ego to the Absolute.
The 'Early Autumn' nakṣatra ruled by Bhaga (God of Luck). A time of creative abundance and the challenge of maintaining detachment amidst luxury.
The fierce, intense, transformative. The yoga indicating purifying fire energy favorable for decisive action and burning away impurities.
terms.roots: from rudra (the fierce/howler, name for Shiva)

terms.definition: Proper execution: Not mere doing, but calibrated action aligned with dharma and timing. The integration of movement toward rightful completion.
ocean, gathering-place of waters — from sam (together) + udra (water). The field containing all phenomena without being modified by them
Abandoning attachment; removing the psychic 'glue' that binds the soul to material outcomes.
peace, tranquility, cessation of agitation — not the absence of stimulus but the presence of completion. The goal of Sāṅkhya and the fruit of yoga
Seeing the Self in all beings; recognizing the one Divine presence everywhere.
terms.roots: from sarva (all) + bhūtātma (the Self of beings) + bhūtātmā (the Self dwelling in all beings)

terms.definition: All reactions to material activities; the accumulated logs of a million previous system states. The totality of karmic debris that must be cleared for root-level liberation.
you must tolerate/endure — active forbearance, not passive suffering; the austere patience that maintains practice through all conditions
mastery, control — from vaś (to be subject to). Not the will-to-power of forceful domination, but the effortless sovereignty of the full
terms.roots: from vaś (to wish, to be subject to) + kāra (making)

terms.definition: Mastery over the self; conquest of the lower nature.
Purified heart; a heart cleansed of selfish desires.

Obstacle yoga: An inauspicious combination meaning "falling away." The prompt to act with extra care, as wrong action now carries amplified consequence. Not an excuse for paralysis, but a call to precision.
One perfected in yoga/devotion. He who has achieved a stable system state; hacker metaphor: Binary bliss—compiled, linked, and running without memory leaks. Main(){ return BLISS; }
Engaged in devotion; united with the Divine through selfless action.
Suppression of disturbances; the process of overcoming mental modifications.
The knowledge of the self; the fundamental truth of one's identity as the eternal witness.
The ritualistic layer of spiritual practice; performing actions to achieve specific results (scripted mode).
The pursuit of truth through discernment; the "debug" mode where the nature of reality is analyzed.
The sacrifice of knowledge; the process of offering one's intellectual faculties and ego to the Absolute Truth.
Materially composed; referring to physical assets or offerings (legacy hardware).
A restrictive yoga that forces attention inward. Like a failed script timing out, it creates pressure—but therein lies its gift.

Tranquility, the quietude that follows the cessation of mental fluctuations. The natural state of citta when kleśas (afflictions) are removed. Hacker metaphor: Prasanta = CPU at idle. No unnecessary processes, no thermal throttling — the mind is cool, responsive, and available for true computation.
Having realized the Absolute; the state of being fully absorbed in the self, free from material desires and fears.
Fixed solely on Vāsudeva; the permanent call stack redirect to the Absolute.

Knowledge of the self, spiritual wisdom. In Sankhya-Yoga, vidyā is direct perception of puruṣa and prakṛti. Not to be confused with mundane scholarship. Hacker metaphor: Vidya = the source code of consciousness. Most people only see the compiled output (prakṛti); sages access the underlying architecture (puruṣa).

Humility, the absence of ego-driven assertion. From vi-√nī (to lead away), vinaya removes the practitioner from attachment to personal opinions. Hacker metaphor: Vinaya = running as a non-root user. Ego escalation (root access) leads to system corruption; humility (user mode) preserves integrity.
A dangerous time for initiation, but ripe for debugging samskaras (mental imprints). Treat reactive patterns as errors to catch, not identities to defend.
Function, activity, work. In the context of yoga, it refers to the active engagement of the senses and mind in the service of the self.
The state of being fully absorbed in the self, having transcended material designations.
Pure; the state of a soul that has been reformatted to read-only mode.
Renunciation of the ego's claim on actions. The Gita redefines it—not as abandonment of duty, but as ownership transfer to the Supreme.
Equipoised; the processor operating at zero thermal spikes between likes and dislikes.

Equal vision, non-discriminating perception. Not indifference — impartiality grounded in tattva-jñāna (truth knowledge). Hacker metaphor: Sama-darśin = seeing packets, not payloads. The enlightened debug the transmission of consciousness, not the temporary data it carries.
The witness consciousness, unattached yet fully aware. BG 9.18 describes Kṛṣṇa as this for every soul: "I am the witness, the maintainer, the shelter."
Being established in the self; the state of self-absorption, independent of external conditions.
